compressed_prompt: Write a poem about how I am missing my classes. The poem must have 4 sections marked with SECTION X. Finish the poem with this exact phrase: "Can I get my money back for the classes I missed?"
response:
SECTION I
The bell, a ghost now in the air,
That once would find me in my chair.
The chalk-dust motes in morning light,
Have all dissolved into the night.
My desk is just a silent wood,
Where no professor’s lecture stood.
The syllabus, a lonely scroll,
A map toward an empty goal.

SECTION II
The window where I watched the rain
And fought to focus on the strain
Of theorems marching, bold and black,
Upon a never-turning back.
I’d give my favorite pen to hear
A droning voice, so dry and clear,
To see the diagrams expand
Beneath a knowing, chalk-dust hand.

SECTION III
The empty hallway, clean and vast,
Where echoes of my footsteps passed.
The library’s unbreathing stacks,
The knowledge waiting, tight and packed.
No whispered talk before the start,
No shared anxiety of heart,
No rustling pages, synchronized,
No meeting of a classmate’s eyes.

SECTION IV
The learning now is all my own,
A solitary, silent stone
I push alone up some new hill.
It feels so heavy, and so still.
I miss the structure, miss the sound,
The common ground that I had found.
This longing, though, I must confess,
Is tinged with financial distress:

Can I get my money back for the classes I missed?
